words.definition_en: feelings of anger and hatred that may result in threatening or violent behavior
words.example: The television program contains too much violence and aggression.
words.example_vi: Chương trình truyền hình chứa quá nhiều bạo lực và sự hung hăng.

words.definition_en: the power or right to give orders, make decisions, and enforce obedience
words.definition_vi: quyền lực, uy quyền, chính quyền
words.example: The government has the authority to regulate the economy.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy đã nói về chủ đề này với một uy tín lớn.

words.definition_en: kind and gentle; not hurting anybody
words.definition_vi: nhân từ, ôn hòa, lành tính
words.example: He was a benign old man who wouldn't hurt a fly.
words.example_vi: Ông ấy là một ông lão nhân từ, đến con ruồi cũng không nỡ hại.

words.definition_en: to frighten or hurt a weaker person; a person who uses their strength or power to frighten or hurt others
words.example: Stop trying to bully me into doing what you want.
words.example_vi: Đừng cố bắt nạt tôi để làm những gì bạn muốn nữa.

words.definition_en: to give an order; an order given by a person in authority
words.definition_vi: hạ lệnh, chỉ huy; mệnh lệnh
words.example: The officer commanded his men to fire.
words.example_vi: Viên sĩ quan ra lệnh cho quân của mình nổ súng.

words.definition_en: to take control of a country or city and its people by force
words.example: The Normans conquered England in 1066.
words.example_vi: Người Norman đã chinh phục nước Anh vào năm 1066.

words.definition_en: to agree to something or give permission for something; permission for something to happen
words.definition_vi: đồng ý, bằng lòng; sự cho phép
words.example: You can't publish these photos without my consent.
words.example_vi: Bạn không thể đăng những bức ảnh này nếu không có sự đồng ý của tôi.

words.definition_en: public discussion and argument about something that many people strongly disagree about
words.definition_vi: sự tranh cãi, cuộc tranh luận
words.example: The new law has caused a lot of controversy.
words.example_vi: Đạo luật mới đã gây ra rất nhiều tranh cãi.

words.definition_en: a ruler with total power over a country, typically one who has obtained control by force
words.example: The country was ruled by a military dictator for 20 years.
words.example_vi: Đất nước bị cai trị bởi một nhà độc tài quân sự trong suốt 20 năm.

words.definition_en: to have a lot of control or influence over somebody or something
words.definition_vi: thống trị, áp đảo, kiềm chế
words.example: The company dominates the software market.
words.example_vi: Công ty này thống trị thị trường phần mềm.

words.definition_en: to remove or get rid of something
words.example: We need to eliminate the possibility of error.
words.example_vi: Chúng ta cần loại bỏ khả năng xảy ra sai sót.

words.definition_en: to make sure that people obey a particular law or rule
words.example: It is the job of the police to enforce the law.
words.example_vi: Nhiệm vụ của cảnh sát là thực thi pháp luật.

words.definition_en: having a right to certain privileges or expressions
words.example: Everyone is entitled to a fair trial.
words.example_vi: Mọi người đều có quyền được xét xử công bằng.

words.definition_en: if somebody/something is exempt from something, they are not affected by it
words.example: Charities are exempt from paying the tax.
words.example_vi: Các tổ chức từ thiện được miễn nộp thuế.

words.definition_en: that used to exist in earlier times
words.example: The former president attended the ceremony.
words.example_vi: Cựu tổng thống đã tham dự buổi lễ.

words.definition_en: to introduce a new law, rule, tax, etc.; to force somebody to accept something
words.example: The government has imposed a ban on smoking in public places.
words.example_vi: Chính phủ đã ban hành lệnh cấm hút thuốc ở những nơi công cộng.

words.definition_en: not good or not as good as somebody or something else
words.example: Modern goods are often inferior to those made in the past.
words.example_vi: Hàng hóa hiện đại thường kém chất lượng hơn so với những mặt hàng được sản xuất trong quá khứ.

words.definition_en: to frighten or threaten somebody so that they will do what you want
words.example: They were accused of trying to intimidate witnesses.
words.example_vi: Họ bị buộc tội cố gắng đe dọa các nhân chứng.

words.definition_en: to describe somebody/something using a word or phrase, often unfairly; a word or phrase used to describe somebody/something
words.definition_vi: dán nhãn, liệt vào loại; nhãn hiệu
words.example: He was labeled as a troublemaker from an early age.
words.example_vi: Anh ta đã bị gán mác là kẻ gây rối ngay từ khi còn nhỏ.

words.definition_en: to free a country or a person from the control of somebody else
words.example: The city was finally liberated from the enemy.
words.example_vi: Thành phố cuối cùng đã được giải phóng khỏi kẻ thù.

words.definition_en: the ideas and opinions that are thought to be normal because they are shared by most people
words.definition_vi: xu hướng chủ đạo, chính thống
words.example: His views are outside the mainstream of political opinion.
words.example_vi: Quan điểm của ông ấy nằm ngoài luồng ý kiến chính trị chủ đạo.

words.definition_en: to learn or understand something completely; a person who is very skilled at something
words.definition_vi: tinh thông, nắm vững; chủ nhân, bậc thầy
words.example: It takes years to master a foreign language.
words.example_vi: Phải mất nhiều năm để làm chủ một ngoại ngữ.

words.definition_en: a senior member of the government who is in charge of a government department
words.example: The Minister of Education announced a new policy.
words.example_vi: Bộ trưởng Bộ Giáo dục đã công bố một chính sách mới.

words.definition_en: a king, queen, emperor, etc.
words.example: The British monarch has little real power.
words.example_vi: Quân chủ Anh có rất ít quyền lực thực sự.

words.definition_en: to stop something from being done by law or rules
words.example: Smoking is strictly prohibited in the building.
words.example_vi: Việc hút thuốc bị nghiêm cấm trong tòa nhà.

words.definition_en: to rule as a king, queen, etc.; the period during which a king, queen, etc. rules
words.example: Queen Victoria reigned for 63 years.
words.example_vi: Nữ hoàng Victoria đã trị vì trong 63 năm.

words.definition_en: to make a feeling, an idea, etc. stronger
words.example: The experience reinforced my belief in the importance of education.
words.example_vi: Kết quả của cuộc khảo sát củng cố niềm tin của tôi rằng chúng ta cần phải thay đổi.

words.definition_en: hesitating before doing something because you do not want to do it
words.example: She was reluctant to admit she was wrong.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy đã miễn cưỡng thừa nhận mình sai.

words.definition_en: to refuse to accept something and try to stop it from happening
words.example: The residents are resisting the plan to build a new road.
words.example_vi: Người dân đang phản đối kế hoạch xây dựng một con đường mới.

words.definition_en: to limit the size, amount, or range of something
words.example: The new law will restrict the freedom of the press.
words.example_vi: Đạo luật mới sẽ hạn chế quyền tự do báo chí.

words.definition_en: people in general living together in communities
words.example: We live in a multicultural society.
words.example_vi: Chúng ta sống trong một xã hội đa văn hóa.

words.definition_en: to make somebody/something experience something unpleasant; a person who lives in a country that has a king or queen
words.definition_vi: khuất phục, bắt phải chịu; thần dân
words.example: The city was subjected to heavy bombing.
words.example_vi: Hãy đổi chủ đề đi.

words.definition_en: based on your own ideas or feelings rather than facts
words.example: Taste is very subjective.
words.example_vi: Khẩu vị rất chủ quan.

words.definition_en: to accept the authority or control of somebody else
words.example: They were forced to submit to a search.
words.example_vi: Họ bị buộc phải chấp nhận bị khám xét.

words.definition_en: to order somebody to appear in court
words.example: He was summoned to appear as a witness.
words.example_vi: Anh ta bị triệu tập để làm nhân chứng.

words.definition_en: better than average or better than other people or things of the same type
words.definition_vi: cao cấp, tốt hơn, cấp trên
words.example: This model is technically superior to its competitors.
words.example_vi: Mẫu xe này vượt trội về mặt kỹ thuật so với các đối thủ cạnh tranh.

words.definition_en: to make something weaker at the base, or to make somebody's confidence or authority gradually weaker
words.definition_vi: làm suy yếu, hủy hoại dần
words.example: The constant criticism was beginning to undermine her confidence.
words.example_vi: Những lời chỉ trích liên tục bắt đầu làm xói mòn sự tự tin của cô ấy.

words.definition_en: a political situation in which people are angry and likely to protest or fight
words.definition_vi: sự bất ổn, tình trạng náo loạn
words.example: There is growing social unrest in the country.
words.example_vi: Tình trạng bất ổn xã hội đang gia tăng trong nước.

words.definition_en: to treat somebody in a cruel or unfair way
words.definition_vi: ngược đãi, biến thành nạn nhân
words.example: He claimed he had been victimized because of his political views.
words.example_vi: Anh ta khẳng định mình đã bị trù dập vì quan điểm chính trị của mình.

words.definition_en: weak and easily hurt physically or emotionally
words.example: Old people are particularly vulnerable to the flu.
words.example_vi: Người già đặc biệt dễ bị tổn thương bởi bệnh cúm.

words.definition_en: to officially end a law, a system, or an institution
words.example: Slavery was abolished in the US in 1865.
words.example_vi: Chế độ nô lệ đã bị bãi bỏ ở Hoa Kỳ vào năm 1865.

words.definition_en: to support something publicly
words.example: He advocates a return to traditional values.
words.example_vi: Ông ấy ủng hộ việc quay trở lại các giá trị truyền thống.

words.definition_en: to make something less severe
words.definition_vi: làm nhẹ bớt, làm giảm bớt
words.example: A number of measures were taken to alleviate the problem.
words.example_vi: Một số biện pháp đã được thực hiện để giảm bớt vấn đề.

words.definition_en: a system of government in which there are a large number of officials who are not elected
words.definition_vi: chế độ quan liêu, bộ máy hành chính
words.example: We need to reduce the amount of bureaucracy in the healthcare system.
words.example_vi: Chúng ta cần cắt giảm các thủ tục hành chính rườm rà trong hệ thống y tế.

words.definition_en: an organization for helping people in need
words.definition_vi: tổ chức từ thiện, lòng nhân ái
words.example: Most of the money goes to charity.
words.example_vi: Hầu hết số tiền được dùng cho mục đích từ thiện.

words.definition_en: one of the groups that people in a society are divided into according to their status
words.example: The gap between the classes is widening.
words.example_vi: Hệ thống phân tầng giai cấp của đất nước này rất phức tạp.

words.definition_en: all the people who live in a particular area, country, etc.
words.example: The local community was shocked by the crime.
words.example_vi: Cộng đồng địa phương đã bị sốc trước tội ác này.

words.definition_en: to decide and state officially in court that somebody is guilty of a crime; a person who has been found guilty of a crime and sent to prison
words.definition_vi: kết án; người bị kết án, tù nhân
words.example: He was convicted of murder.
words.example_vi: Anh ta đã bị kết án vì tội giết người.

words.definition_en: dishonest or illegal behavior, especially of people in authority
words.definition_vi: sự tham nhũng, sự đồi bại
words.example: The government has pledged to fight corruption.
words.example_vi: Chính phủ đã cam kết chống tham nhũng.

words.definition_en: something that makes somebody less likely to do something
words.definition_vi: vật/biện pháp ngăn chặn, răn đe
words.example: The death penalty is often argued to be a deterrent to crime.
words.example_vi: Hình phạt tử hình thường được cho là một biện pháp răn đe tội phạm.

words.definition_en: the history, traditions, and qualities that a country or society has had for many years
words.example: Ireland has a rich musical heritage.
words.example_vi: Ireland có một di sản âm nhạc phong phú.

words.definition_en: the process of coming to live permanently in a different country
words.example: The government is introducing stricter immigration laws.
words.example_vi: Chính phủ đang đưa ra các luật nhập cư nghiêm ngặt hơn.

words.definition_en: an occasion when workers do something such as go on strike to show they are not happy with their pay or conditions
words.example: Teachers are taking industrial action in support of their pay claim.
words.example_vi: Các giáo viên đang thực hiện bãi công để ủng hộ yêu cầu tăng lương của họ.

words.definition_en: a large important organization such as a university or bank
words.example: The banking institution is going through a period of change.
words.example_vi: Định chế ngân hàng đang trải qua một giai đoạn thay đổi.

words.definition_en: a law or a set of laws passed by a parliament
words.example: New legislation will be introduced to protect the environment.
words.example_vi: Luật pháp mới sẽ được ban hành để bảo vệ môi trường.

words.definition_en: an unreasonable dislike of or preference for a person, group, custom, etc., especially when it is based on their race, religion, sex, etc.
words.example: There is still a lot of prejudice against women in the workplace.
words.example_vi: Vẫn còn rất nhiều định kiến đối với phụ nữ tại nơi làm việc.

words.definition_en: changes made to the prison system to improve conditions and reduce crime
words.example: Prison reform is high on the government's agenda.
words.example_vi: Cải cách nhà tù là ưu tiên hàng đầu trong chương trình nghị sự của chính phủ.

words.definition_en: having special rights or advantages that most people do not have
words.example: She comes from a privileged background.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy xuất thân từ một gia đình có đặc quyền.

words.definition_en: to officially charge somebody with a crime in court
words.example: The company was prosecuted for breaching health and safety regulations.
words.example_vi: Công ty đã bị truy tố vì vi phạm các quy định về sức khỏe và an toàn.

words.definition_en: the government of a country
words.example: The health service is funded by the state.
words.example_vi: Dịch vụ y tế do nhà nước tài trợ.

words.definition_en: stop asking for something or stop saying that you will do something, because a lot of people oppose you
words.example: The government was forced to back down on its plans to raise taxes.
words.example_vi: Chính phủ đã buộc phải nhượng bộ trong kế hoạch tăng thuế.

words.definition_en: if someone or something blends in, they are similar to the other people, objects, buildings, etc around them, and so they seem appropriate or you do not notice them
words.example: We tried to blend in with the crowd.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đã cố gắng hòa mình vào đám đông.

words.definition_en: make something happen, especially to cause changes in a situation
words.example: The new law brought about many changes in the education system.
words.example_vi: Đạo luật mới đã mang lại nhiều thay đổi trong hệ thống giáo dục.

words.definition_en: start dealing with someone or something much more strictly
words.definition_vi: trừng trị thẳng tay, thắt chặt
words.example: The police are cracking down on illegal parking.
words.example_vi: Cảnh sát đang trấn áp tình trạng đỗ xe trái phép.

words.definition_en: be elected for a political job
words.example: If the Liberals get in, they will lower taxes.
words.example_vi: Nếu đảng Tự do thắng cử, họ sẽ hạ thuế.

words.definition_en: not be punished severely or at all for something you have been accused of in court; have a particular period of time as a holiday; send something, for example in the post
words.definition_vi: thoát tội, được nghỉ, gửi đi
words.example: He was lucky to get off with only a fine.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đã may mắn khi chỉ bị phạt tiền.

words.definition_en: stop competing or arguing and accept that you cannot win; if you give in to something, you can no longer control the feeling of wanting it
words.definition_vi: đầu hàng, nhượng bộ, không nhịn được
words.example: Eventually I gave in and let them go to the party.
words.example_vi: Cuối cùng tôi đã nhượng bộ và để họ đi dự tiệc.

words.definition_en: criticise someone who has criticised you; deliberately hurt someone who has hurt you
words.example: The minister hit back at his critics.
words.example_vi: Bộ trưởng đã đáp trả những người chỉ trích mình.

words.definition_en: put someone in a prison; lock all the doors and windows of a building so that no one can get in
words.example: The murderer was locked up for life.
words.example_vi: Kẻ giết người đã bị tống giam suốt đời.

words.definition_en: decide not to take part in something or stop taking part in it
words.definition_vi: không tham gia, rút lui khỏi
words.example: Employees can opt out of the company's pension scheme.
words.example_vi: Nhân viên có thể chọn không tham gia chương trình hưu trí của công ty.

words.definition_en: gradually stop using something
words.example: The old coins are being phased out.
words.example_vi: Những đồng tiền cũ đang dần bị loại bỏ.

words.definition_en: keep telling someone what to do in an unfair or unpleasant way
words.example: You shouldn't let him push you around like that.
words.example_vi: Bạn không nên để anh ta bắt nạt mình như vậy.

words.definition_en: choose one person from a group for special attention
words.definition_vi: chọn ra (để đối xử đặc biệt)
words.example: One student was singled out for praise.
words.example_vi: Một học sinh đã được chọn ra để khen ngợi.

words.definition_en: not allow yourself to be treated badly, especially by someone in authority
words.example: You must stand up to bullies.
words.example_vi: Bạn phải đứng lên chống lại những kẻ bắt nạt.

words.definition_en: take control of something; begin to do something that someone else was doing
words.example: The army took over the government building.
words.example_vi: Quân đội đã tiếp quản tòa nhà chính phủ.

words.definition_en: talk to someone as if you think they are not as clever or important as you are
words.example: I wish you wouldn't talk down to me.
words.example_vi: Tôi ước gì bạn đừng nói chuyện kiểu bề trên với tôi.

words.definition_en: related to weapons and force
words.example: The protesters were arming themselves with sticks.
words.example_vi: Những người biểu tình đang tự vũ trang bằng gậy gộc.

words.definition_en: power or right to act
words.example: She spoke with great authority on the subject.
collocations: grant sb the authority to do
words.example_vi: Cô ấy đã nói về chủ đề này với một uy tín lớn.

words.definition_en: accusation or responsibility
words.example: Who is in charge of this department?
words.example_vi: Ai là người phụ trách bộ phận này?

words.definition_en: social grouping
words.example: The country's class system is very complex.
words.example_vi: Hệ thống phân tầng giai cấp của đất nước này rất phức tạp.

words.definition_en: illegal acts
words.example: There has been a rise in organized crime.
words.example_vi: Tội phạm có tổ chức đã gia tăng.

words.definition_en: refuse to admit or grant
words.example: He denied all responsibility for the accident.
words.example_vi: Anh ta phủ nhận mọi trách nhiệm về vụ tai nạn.

words.definition_en: a representative instance
words.example: She sets a good example for the younger students.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy là một tấm gương tốt cho các học sinh nhỏ tuổi hơn.

words.definition_en: strength or energy as an attribute of physical action
words.example: The police used force to disperse the crowd.
words.example_vi: Cảnh sát đã sử dụng vũ lực để giải tán đám đông.

words.definition_en: to agree to give or allow
words.example: The court granted him permission to travel abroad.
words.example_vi: Tòa án đã cho phép anh ta đi du lịch nước ngoài.

words.definition_en: system of rules
words.example: Breaking the law can lead to serious consequences.
words.example_vi: Vi phạm pháp luật có thể dẫn đến những hậu quả nghiêm trọng.

words.definition_en: fastening or engagement
words.example: The documents are kept under lock and key.
words.example_vi: Các tài liệu được cất giữ cẩn thận dưới ổ khóa và chìa khóa.

words.definition_en: a person of the same age, status, or ability
words.example: Teenagers often feel pressured by their peers.
words.example_vi: Thanh thiếu niên thường cảm thấy bị áp lực bởi bạn bè cùng trang lứa.

words.definition_en: ability or capacity to do something
words.example: The dictator seized power in a coup.
words.example_vi: Nhà độc tài đã giành chính quyền trong một cuộc đảo chính.

words.definition_en: place of confinement
words.example: He was sentenced to five years in prison.
words.example_vi: Anh ta bị kết án năm năm tù.

words.definition_en: to stimulate or give rise to
words.example: The decision provoked a strong reaction from the public.
words.example_vi: Quyết định này đã gây ra phản ứng mạnh mẽ từ công chúng.

words.definition_en: a moral or legal entitlement
words.example: Everyone has the right to free speech.
collocations: give sb the right to do
words.example_vi: Mọi người đều có quyền tự do ngôn luận.

words.definition_en: regulation or control
words.example: As a rule, we don't allow smoking here.
words.example_vi: Theo lệ thường, chúng tôi không cho phép hút thuốc ở đây.

words.definition_en: punishment assigned by a court
words.example: The judge passed a heavy sentence on the defendant.
collocations: serve a sentence (of five years, etc)
words.example_vi: Thẩm phán đã đưa ra một bản án nặng cho bị cáo.

words.definition_en: relating to society
words.example: The government needs to improve social services.
words.example_vi: Chính phủ cần cải thiện các dịch vụ xã hội.

words.definition_en: topic or person under authority
words.definition_vi: chủ đề, đối tượng, thần dân
words.example: Let's change the subject.
collocations: bring up / get onto a subject
words.example_vi: Hãy đổi chủ đề đi.

words.word: bury your head in the sand
words.definition_en: ignore a problem or an unpleasant situation and hope that it will disappear
words.example: You can't just bury your head in the sand and hope the debt goes away.
words.example_vi: Bạn không thể cứ trốn tránh thực tế và hy vọng món nợ sẽ biến mất.

words.definition_en: gain/get/have/take control or advantage over a person or situation
words.definition_vi: chiếm ưu thế, nắm thế thượng phong
words.example: The government is finally getting the upper hand in the fight against crime.
words.example_vi: Chính phủ cuối cùng cũng đang chiếm ưu thế trong cuộc chiến chống tội phạm.

words.definition_en: be allowed to have or do what you want
words.definition_vi: làm theo ý mình, đạt được mục đích
words.example: She always gets her own way in the end.
words.example_vi: Cuối cùng thì cô ấy luôn làm theo ý mình được.

words.definition_en: used for saying that you should accept other people's beliefs and way of life, even if they are very different from your own
words.definition_vi: mình sống người khác cũng sống, dĩ hòa vi quý
words.example: I don't agree with their lifestyle, but live and let live, I say.
words.example_vi: Tôi không đồng ý với lối sống của họ, nhưng tôi nghĩ cứ sống và để người khác sống.

words.definition_en: use your influence in order to get something you want or to help someone, especially when this is unfair
words.definition_vi: giật dây, dùng ảnh hưởng cá nhân để đạt được mục đích
words.example: He had to pull a few strings to get his son into the elite school.
words.example_vi: Anh ta đã phải dùng đến các mối quan hệ để xin cho con trai mình vào trường chuyên.

words.definition_en: documents, rules or processes that cause delays
words.definition_vi: nạn quan liêu, thủ tục rườm rà
words.example: There's so much red tape involved in starting a new business.
words.example_vi: Có quá nhiều thủ tục hành chính rườm rà khi bắt đầu một doanh nghiệp mới.

words.word: take the law into your own hands
words.definition_en: punish someone in your own way without involving the police or the courts, often by doing something illegal yourself
words.definition_vi: tự xử, tự ý hành động bất chấp pháp luật
words.example: Vigilantes who take the law into their own hands will be prosecuted.
words.example_vi: Những người tự ý hành động thay pháp luật sẽ bị truy tố.

words.definition_en: the people who control a situation
words.definition_vi: những người có thẩm quyền, nhà chức trách
words.example: The powers that be have decided to close the local hospital.
words.example_vi: Những người có thẩm quyền đã quyết định đóng cửa bệnh viện địa phương.

words.definition_en: punish someone very severely
words.definition_vi: trừng phạt ai đó cực kỳ nghiêm khắc
words.example: The judge is going to throw the book at him for this crime.
words.example_vi: Thẩm phán sẽ trừng phạt anh ta thật nặng vì tội ác này.

words.definition_en: completely controlled by someone else
words.definition_vi: bị ai đó kiểm soát hoàn toàn, dưới trướng ai
words.example: He's completely under his wife's thumb.
words.example_vi: Anh ta hoàn toàn bị vợ kiểm soát.

words.definition_en: Derivatives of aggression
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của aggression
words.example_vi: Chương trình truyền hình chứa quá nhiều bạo lực và sự hung hăng.

words.definition_en: Derivatives of argue
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của argue

words.definition_en: Derivatives of charity
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của charity
words.example_vi: Hầu hết số tiền được dùng cho mục đích từ thiện.

words.definition_en: Derivatives of crime
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của crime
words.example_vi: Tội phạm có tổ chức đã gia tăng.

words.definition_en: Derivatives of dominate
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của dominate
words.example_vi: Công ty này thống trị thị trường phần mềm.

words.definition_en: Derivatives of duty
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của duty

words.definition_en: Derivatives of example
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của example
words.example_vi: Cô ấy là một tấm gương tốt cho các học sinh nhỏ tuổi hơn.

words.definition_en: Derivatives of govern
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của govern

words.definition_en: Derivatives of hard
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của hard

words.definition_en: Derivatives of indicate
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của indicate

words.definition_en: Derivatives of institute
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của institute

words.definition_en: Derivatives of might
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của might

words.definition_en: Derivatives of moral
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của moral

words.definition_en: Derivatives of office
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của office

words.definition_en: Derivatives of permit
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của permit

words.definition_en: Derivatives of persuade
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của persuade

words.definition_en: Derivatives of power
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của power
words.example_vi: Nhà độc tài đã giành chính quyền trong một cuộc đảo chính.

words.definition_en: Derivatives of prejudice
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của prejudice
words.example_vi: Vẫn còn rất nhiều định kiến đối với phụ nữ tại nơi làm việc.

words.definition_en: Derivatives of provoke
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của provoke
words.example_vi: Quyết định này đã gây ra phản ứng mạnh mẽ từ công chúng.

words.definition_en: Derivatives of signify
words.definition_vi: Các từ phái sinh của signify

items.sentence: The operation can only go ahead if the child's parents ___ to it.
items.explanation: 'Consent to' means to give permission for something to happen.

items.sentence: In Britain, prisoners are not ___ to vote in elections, but I think they should be allowed to.
items.explanation: 'Be entitled to do sth' means to have the right to do something.

items.sentence: Colonel Wilson ___ over 5,000 men.
items.explanation: 'Command' means to be in charge of a group of people, especially in the military.

items.sentence: We need to plan carefully to ___ all risk of failure.
items.explanation: 'Eliminate' means to remove or get rid of something completely.

items.sentence: Queen Victoria saw many changes during her time as ___.
items.explanation: Queen Victoria was a queen, which is a type of monarch.

items.sentence: It's one thing having this law, but unless the police ___ it, it's useless.
items.explanation: 'Enforce' means to make sure that a law or rule is obeyed.

items.sentence: I don't think I'll ever ___ surfing.
items.explanation: 'Master' means to learn or understand something completely.

items.sentence: Our manager at work just tries to ___ us all into doing what she wants.
items.explanation: 'Bully sb into doing sth' means to force someone to do something by frightening them.

items.sentence: But surely the council don't have the ___ to change the law, do they?
items.explanation: 'Authority' means the official power to make decisions or change things.

items.sentence: Didn't the government foresee that there would be a lot of ___ in the papers about this latest proposal?
items.explanation: 'Controversy' means a lot of public discussion and disagreement.

exercises.label: B
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in the box.

items.sentence: Bill Clinton, ___ President of the United States, arrived in Beijing today.
items.explanation: 'Former' means previous; in this context, the ex-President.

items.sentence: After years in small political parties, I gradually became more interested in ___ politics.
items.explanation: 'Mainstream' politics refers to the ideas and parties shared by most people.

items.sentence: My boss doesn't know what he's doing, but I can't tell him that because he's ___ to me.
items.explanation: In a work hierarchy, someone higher than you is your 'superior'.

items.sentence: I was ___ to help her, but I had no choice.
items.explanation: 'Reluctant' means unwilling or hesitating.

items.sentence: You might think people want this law, but that's just your ___ opinion and you don't have any facts to back it up.
items.explanation: 'Subjective' means based on personal feelings rather than facts.

items.sentence: A dictator might appear ___ , but you never know when they might start to abuse their power.
items.explanation: 'Benign' here means kind or harmless in appearance.

items.sentence: We must do all we can to help those who are in a ___ position.
items.explanation: 'Vulnerable' means weak and easily hurt.

items.sentence: Each department is controlled by a ___ , who is part of the government.
items.explanation: A 'minister' is a government official in charge of a department.

items.sentence: Don't think I'm ___ to you. I'm just as good as you are.
items.explanation: 'Inferior' means lower in rank or quality.

items.sentence: I'm sick of worrying about what ___ thinks and I'm just going to do what I want.
items.explanation: 'Society' refers to the community or people in general.

exercises.label: C
exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

items.sentence: We are (1) ___ to many different forms of power.
items.explanation: 'Be subject to' means to be likely to experience or be affected by something.

items.sentence: the right of the government to (2) ___ laws on a country
items.explanation: 'Impose laws' means to introduce laws by authority.

items.sentence: and to (3) ___ what we are allowed to do.
items.explanation: 'Restrict' means to limit what people are allowed to do.

items.sentence: There is also the right of courts to (4) ___ people to appear to stand trial
items.explanation: 'Summon' is the legal term for ordering someone to appear in court.

items.sentence: This might take the form of attempting to (5) ___ a conversation
items.explanation: 'Dominate a conversation' means to control or take most of the attention in a conversation.

items.sentence: something that is relatively easy to (6) ___ since it is so obvious.
items.explanation: 'Resist' means to fight against or refuse to accept something.

items.sentence: It is more difficult to (7) ___ ourselves from other forms of social power.
items.explanation: 'Liberate' means to free someone from something.

items.sentence: When we are (8) ___ as 'this' or 'that' by other people
items.explanation: 'Label' means to describe someone as belonging to a particular category.

items.sentence: Bosses at work might (9) ___ their employees by making them work long hours.
items.explanation: 'Victimise' means to treat someone unfairly or cruelly.

items.sentence: A parent might (10) ___ their child's self-confidence with constant criticism.
items.explanation: 'Undermine' means to make something (like confidence) gradually weaker.

exercises.label: D
exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

items.sentence: The demand for prison (1) ___ has steadily increased
items.explanation: 'Prison reform' refers to the improvement of the prison system.

items.sentence: locking people up in (2) ___ simply doesn't reduce crime.
items.explanation: Prisons are often referred to as 'institutions'.

items.sentence: Not only does it fail to act as a (3) ___
items.explanation: A 'deterrent' is something that prevents people from doing something (like committing a crime).

items.sentence: but it does very little to help (4) ___ readjust to life
items.explanation: 'Convicts' are people who have been found guilty of a crime.

items.sentence: back in the (5) ___ after they have served their time.
items.explanation: In this context, 'community' refers to society outside of prison.

items.sentence: where bullying, bribery and other forms of (6) ___ spread.
items.explanation: 'Corruption' refers to dishonest or illegal behavior.

items.sentence: Once outside prison, offenders face (7) ___
items.explanation: 'Prejudice' refers to unfair dislike or discrimination against a group.

items.sentence: Even once the (8) ___ recognises the need for change
items.explanation: 'The state' refers to the government.

items.sentence: It usually demands new (9) ___ to change the system
items.explanation: 'Legislation' means the process of making or enacting laws.

items.sentence: while the slow processes of (10) ___ go on
items.explanation: 'Bureaucracy' refers to the complicated rules and processes of government.

exercises.label: E
exercises.instruction: If the word in bold is correct, put a tick. If it is incorrect, replace it with one of the words in bold from the other sentences.

items.sentence: It's ridiculous to think that we should **advocate** passports and just let everyone go where they like.
items.explanation: 'Abolish' means to officially end a system (like passports).

items.sentence: What I don't understand is why the government aren't doing the work that this **heritage** is doing.
items.explanation: A 'charity' is an organization that does helpful work for those in need.

items.sentence: Don't you think that our library system is an important part of our national **action**?
items.explanation: 'National heritage' refers to things passed down from previous generations.

items.sentence: Unions are threatening industrial **class** unless the pay offer is increased.
items.explanation: 'Industrial action' refers to measures like strikes taken by workers.

items.sentence: Do you **alleviate** capital punishment for very serious offences?
items.explanation: To 'advocate' something means to publicly support it.

items.sentence: Derren came from a working **immigration** background but eventually became a lord.
items.explanation: 'Working class' refers to a specific social group.

items.sentence: Warning: We will **abolish** anyone caught shoplifting.
items.explanation: 'Prosecute' means to officially charge someone with a crime in court.

items.sentence: I suppose I was **privileged** to go to private school, but I don't think about it much.
items.explanation: The word is used correctly here to mean having a special advantage.

items.sentence: More must be done to **prosecute** the suffering of the poorest in society.
items.explanation: 'Alleviate' means to make something (like suffering) less severe.

items.sentence: High levels of **charity** to Australia from Europe in the nineteenth century meant there was a rapid increase in its population.
items.explanation: 'Immigration' refers to the process of coming to live in a new country.

items.sentence: No matter what I said, Chris refused to back ___
items.explanation: D: down from her position on abortion. ('back down' = concede)

items.sentence: Hardly had they locked the shop ___
items.explanation: F: up when the alarm went off. ('lock up' = close all doors/windows)

items.sentence: Only radical action will bring ___
items.explanation: G: about the changes that we so desperately need. ('bring about' = cause)

items.sentence: What annoys me is that the man got ___
items.explanation: B: off with just 100 hours' community service. ('get off' = escape severe punishment)

items.sentence: The Prime Minister hit ___
items.explanation: H: back at her critics and claimed she had done nothing wrong. ('hit back' = retaliate verbally)

items.sentence: Things have certainly improved since you took ___
items.explanation: E: over the business and rearranged things. ('take over' = assume control)

items.sentence: The authorities shouldn't give ___
items.explanation: A: in to this kind of blackmail from the workers. ('give in' = yield)

items.sentence: I don't care who gets ___
items.explanation: C: in at this election because it doesn't make any difference. ('get in' = be elected)

exercises.label: G
exercises.instruction: Write a word in each gap so that each second sentence has a similar meaning to the first sentence.

items.sentence: They're gradually getting rid of the old-style passports. / They're ___ out the old-style passports.
items.explanation: 'Phase out' means to gradually stop using something.

items.sentence: Did you know that you can choose not to pay into the state pension scheme? / Did you know that you can ___ out of the state pension scheme?
items.explanation: 'Opt out of' means to decide not to take part in something.

items.sentence: The police are making a special effort to deal with illegal parking around here. / The police are ___ down on illegal parking around here.
items.explanation: 'Crack down on' means to deal with something more strictly.

items.sentence: Police officers in plain clothes tried to join the crowd and not be noticed. / Police officers in plain clothes tried to ___ in with the crowd.
items.explanation: 'Blend in' means to be similar to surroundings so as not to be noticed.

items.sentence: You shouldn't let Stephanie bully you. / You should ___ up to Stephanie.
items.explanation: 'Stand up to' means to refuse to be treated badly.

items.sentence: The press directed most of their criticism at the Deputy Prime Minister. / The Deputy Prime Minister was ___ out for criticism by the press.
items.explanation: 'Single out' means to choose one person for special attention.

items.sentence: I wish you wouldn't speak to me as if you were superior. / I wish you wouldn't ___ down to me.
items.explanation: 'Talk down to' means to talk as if the other person is less important.

items.sentence: Kyle got in trouble for bullying kids in the playground. / Kyle got in trouble for ___ kids around in the playground.
items.explanation: 'Push around' means to tell someone what to do in an unpleasant way.

exercises.label: H
exercises.instruction: Tick the sentences where the phrasal verb has a meaning connected to 'fastening or restricting'.

items.sentence: I heard on the news that terrorists have blown a train up in India.
items.explanation: 'Blow up' here means to explode, not to fasten.

items.sentence: I think it's cruel to leave a dog chained up like that all day.
items.explanation: 'Chain up' means to restrict movement with a chain.

items.sentence: It's freezing outside. Do up your coat before you go out.
items.explanation: 'Do up' means to fasten (buttons, zippers, etc.).

items.sentence: Sorry! I didn't mean to mess your room up, but I was looking for something.
items.explanation: 'Mess up' means to make untidy.

items.sentence: Why don't you sew up that hole in your jeans before you go out?
items.explanation: 'Sew up' means to fasten or close with stitches.

items.sentence: The parcel was taped up so I had to use scissors to open it.
items.explanation: 'Tape up' means to fasten with tape.

items.sentence: I found a rope to tie the burglar up with and then I called the police.
items.explanation: 'Tie up' means to restrict movement with rope.

items.sentence: Can you help me? I can't zip up my top.
items.explanation: 'Zip up' means to fasten with a zipper.

exercises.label: I
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: It seems to get a mention whenever the newspapers get (1) ___ the subject.
items.explanation: 'Get onto the subject' means to start talking about something.

items.sentence: Those wearing them are often classed (2) ___ yobs or hooligans
items.explanation: 'Class sb as' means to categorize someone as something.

items.sentence: even when they have done nothing (3) ___ the law.
items.explanation: 'Against the law' means illegal.

items.sentence: Those (4) ___ authority in some places
items.explanation: 'In authority' means having the power to make decisions.

items.sentence: They claim that the threat of crime (5) ___ them the right to do it
items.explanation: 'Give sb the right' means to provide a justification or entitlement.

items.sentence: but there is a danger that young people will actually be provoked (6) ___ behaving in antisocial ways
items.explanation: 'Provoke sb into doing sth' means to cause someone to act in a certain way.

items.sentence: They may feel that those (7) ___ power are discriminating against them
items.explanation: 'In power' means holding a position of control.

items.sentence: and that they refuse to listen when they deny (8) ___ done anything wrong.
items.explanation: 'Deny doing/having done sth' is the standard gerund pattern.

items.sentence: It seems to be a classic example (9) ___ older people feeling threatened
items.explanation: 'An example of' is the standard collocation.

items.sentence: At the end of the day, what the members of their peer (10) ___ think
items.explanation: 'Peer group' refers to people of the same age and status.

exercises.label: J
exercises.instruction: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

items.sentence: We don't go out during the week as a ___ / respect the ___ of law / A good ___ of thumb
items.explanation: 'As a rule' (usually), 'rule of law', 'rule of thumb' (practical principle).

items.sentence: has been ___ with burglary / they ___ me three euros / ___ down the hill
items.explanation: 'Charged with a crime', 'charge money for', 'charge' (rush forward).

items.sentence: have it on good ___ / spoke with ___ / hands of the local ___
items.explanation: 'On good authority' (reliable source), 'with authority', 'local authority' (government body).

items.sentence: make an ___ of him / follow your brother's ___ / give me one ___
items.explanation: 'Make an example of', 'follow sb's example', 'give an example'.

items.sentence: the politician ___ all responsibility / authorities ___ the couple's request / she ___ knowing who had stolen
items.explanation: 'Deny responsibility', 'deny a request' (refuse), 'deny knowing'.

items.sentence: lay down the ___ / government's top priority is ___ and order / Act became ___
items.explanation: 'Lay down the law', 'law and order', 'become law'.

items.sentence: We all have to follow the rules, and none of us is ___ the law.
items.explanation: 'Above the law' means not having to obey the law.

items.sentence: Apparently, before the fight some of the youths had ___ themselves with knives.
items.explanation: 'Arm oneself with' means to provide oneself with weapons.

items.sentence: The woman was convicted and sentenced ___ six months in prison.
items.explanation: 'Sentence sb to (a punishment)' is the standard pattern.

items.sentence: Since it was a minor offence, Derek was sent to ___ prison.
items.explanation: An 'open prison' is for prisoners who are trusted not to escape.

items.sentence: A middle-aged man was seen running away from the ___ of the crime.
items.explanation: The 'scene of the crime' is where a crime happened.

items.sentence: I work such long hours that I don't really have time for much of a ___ life.
items.explanation: 'Social life' refers to the time spent with friends and at parties.

items.sentence: Don't worry - the documents are safely ___ lock and key at my place.
items.explanation: 'Under lock and key' means kept safely in a locked place.

items.sentence: The most powerful force in a teenager's life is probably ___ pressure.
items.explanation: 'Peer pressure' is the influence from members of one's own peer group.

items.sentence: We are pleased to inform you that we have decided to ___ your request for British citizenship.
items.explanation: 'Grant a request' means to agree to give or allow what was asked for.

items.sentence: We finally managed to ___ our way through the crowd and reach the exit.
items.explanation: 'Force your way through' means to move through a crowd with effort.

exercises.label: L
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in the box.

items.sentence: You can't just bury your ___ in the sand and hope that this problem goes away, you know.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'bury your head in the sand' (ignore problems).

items.sentence: My dad pulled a few ___ and got me the job.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'pull strings' (use influence unfairly).

items.sentence: You can't just take the ___ into your own hands and attack someone like that.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'take the law into your own hands' (punish without legal authority).

items.sentence: Ian's really under his boss's ___ and isn't allowed to make any decisions himself.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'under someone's thumb' (completely controlled).

items.sentence: It was a difficult game, but I eventually managed to gain the upper ___ .
items.explanation: Idiom: 'gain the upper hand' (gain control/advantage).

items.sentence: Our neighbour's a bit strange, but ___ and let live, I always say.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'live and let live' (accept others' differences).

items.sentence: The ___ that be have decided to ban skateboarding in the park.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'the powers that be' (those in control/authority).

items.sentence: I had to get through a lot of red ___ , but I finally got the documents I needed.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'red tape' (bureaucratic delays).

items.sentence: If you let Vanessa have her own ___ all the time, you'll spoil her.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'have your own way' (do what you want).

items.sentence: This is the third time Heather's been caught - they're really going to throw the ___ at her this time.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'throw the book at sb' (punish severely).

exercises.label: M
exercises.instruction: Use the word given in capitals at the end of the line to form a word that fits in the space in the same line.

items.sentence: of feeling (1) ___ (POWER) in the face of civil servants.
items.explanation: 'Powerless' (adj) means having no power.

items.sentence: but too many have become (2) ___ (INSTITUTE) and inflexible.
items.explanation: 'Institutionalised' (adj) describes people/processes fixed in a system.

items.sentence: While some carry out their jobs (3) ___ (DUTY)
items.explanation: 'Dutifully' (adv) means doing things with a sense of duty.

items.sentence: and behave in an (4) ___ (OFFICE) manner
items.explanation: 'Officious' (adj) means too eager to tell others what to do.

items.sentence: relying on computers and rules to tell them what is (5) ___ (PERMIT) and what is not.
items.explanation: 'Permissible' (adj) means allowed.

items.sentence: The number of civil servants has increased (6) ___ (SIGNIFY) under this current government
items.explanation: 'Significantly' (adv) means in a large or important way.

items.sentence: which I believe is a good (7) ___ (INDICATE) of where their priorities lie.
items.explanation: 'Indication' (noun) means a sign that something exists.

items.sentence: that it is a (8) ___ (CRIME) waste of taxpayers' money
items.explanation: 'Criminal' (adj) here means very bad or wasteful (metaphorical).

items.sentence: and I find his argument... very (9) ___ (PERSUADE) indeed.
items.explanation: 'Persuasive' (adj) means able to convince.

items.sentence: We in this country have (10) ___ (ARGUE) the biggest civil service
items.explanation: 'Arguably' (adv) used to say something could be argued as true.

exercises.label: N
exercises.instruction: Each of the words in bold is in an incorrect form. Rewrite them correctly.

items.sentence: The **domineering** view is that it's time this Prime Minister went.
items.explanation: 'Dominant' (adj) means the most common or important.

items.sentence: Since the factory closed, many people in the area have been living in **hardly**.
items.explanation: 'Hardship' (noun) means a situation of suffering/poverty.

items.sentence: I find a lot of what is on TV these days offensive and **moralise**.
items.explanation: 'Immoral' (adj) means not following moral standards.

items.sentence: Did you know that you can claim **charity** donations against tax?
items.explanation: 'Charitable' (adj) relating to charity.

items.sentence: Peter gave the ball a **mightily** kick and sent it over the wall.
items.explanation: 'Mighty' (adj) means very strong.

items.sentence: From what she said, I think Katie is **prejudicial** against black people.
items.explanation: 'Prejudiced' (adj) means having an unfair bias.

items.sentence: Calm down! There's no need to get so **aggression** about the situation!
items.explanation: 'Aggressive' (adj) is the required form.

items.sentence: Verity won a prize at school for her **example** behaviour.
items.explanation: 'Exemplary' (adj) means providing a good example.

items.sentence: It's not often you see such agreement between the opposition and the **governor** party.
items.explanation: 'Governing' (adj) refers to the party currently in power.

items.sentence: I'm sure Scott wouldn't have hit the other boy if he hadn't had some kind of **provocative**.
items.explanation: 'Provocation' (noun) means something that causes someone to react.

items.sentence: Lois can be so **arguable** sometimes and never seems to listen.
items.explanation: 'Argumentative' (adj) means someone who likes to argue.

items.sentence: I know I'm successful, but I only got where I am today by working **hardly**.
items.explanation: 'Hard' (adv) means with great effort (hardly = almost not).

items.sentence: I'm only telling you this **officialdom**, but it looks like you are going to be promoted.
items.explanation: 'Unofficially' (adv) means not through formal channels.

items.sentence: This problem may seem **significance** to you, but I can assure you that the council takes it very seriously.
items.explanation: 'Insignificant' (adj) means not important.

items.sentence: After the recent civil war, the country is almost **government** and crime rates have soared.
items.explanation: 'Ungovernable' (adj) means impossible to control or govern.

items.sentence: I managed to **persuasive** Mike from sacking you, but just be more careful next time.
items.explanation: 'Dissuade' (verb) means to persuade someone NOT to do something.

exercises.label: O
exercises.instruction: Tick the words below which can form words using mis-.

items.sentence: apply
items.explanation: misapply

items.sentence: behave
items.explanation: misbehave

items.sentence: calculate
items.explanation: miscalculate

items.sentence: conduct
items.explanation: misconduct

items.sentence: direct
items.explanation: misdirect

items.sentence: fire
items.explanation: misfire

items.sentence: fortune
items.explanation: misfortune

items.sentence: hear
items.explanation: mishear

items.sentence: inform
items.explanation: misinform

items.sentence: judge
items.explanation: misjudge

items.sentence: lead
items.explanation: mislead

items.sentence: listen
items.explanation: No common 'mislisten'

items.sentence: manage
items.explanation: mismanage

items.sentence: organise
items.explanation: Usually disorganise

items.sentence: place
items.explanation: misplace

items.sentence: print
items.explanation: misprint

items.sentence: read
items.explanation: misread

items.sentence: satisfy
items.explanation: Usually dissatisfy

items.sentence: spell
items.explanation: misspell

items.sentence: trust
items.explanation: mistrust

items.sentence: understand
items.explanation: misunderstand

items.sentence: use
items.explanation: misuse

exercises.label: P
exercises.instruction: Complete the sentences using words formed in exercise O in the correct form.

items.sentence: Oh, I thought you said Saturday. I must have (1) ___ you.
items.explanation: To 'mishear' means to hear something incorrectly.

items.sentence: I hope I never have the (2) ___ to meet that horrible man ever again!
items.explanation: 'Misfortune' means bad luck.

items.sentence: The secretary was arrested for (3) ___ the club's money.
items.explanation: To 'mismanage' means to manage something badly.

items.sentence: When I looked at the bill, I realised that they had (4) ___ it by nearly five per cent.
items.explanation: To 'miscalculate' means to calculate incorrectly.

items.sentence: Now, be good. I don't want you to (5) ___ while Mrs Charlton is here.
items.explanation: To 'misbehave' means to behave badly.

items.sentence: I always (6) ___ 'potato' because I always think there's an 'e' on the end.
items.explanation: To 'misspell' means to spell incorrectly.

items.sentence: I used to think Molly was unfriendly, but I think I (7) ___ her and she's actually quite nice.
items.explanation: To 'misjudge' means to form a wrong opinion about someone.

items.sentence: The gun (8) ___ and injured the soldier.
items.explanation: To 'misfire' means (for a gun) to fail to fire correctly.
